texto: Every working mother has the things she dreads, things that keep her up in the night – pink eye, an ear infection, the parent-teacher conference, the school play – all forcing her to remind the people she works with that she is not, in fact, wholly devoted to business enterprises, but has another secret life. For me, the night terror is the 5 a.m. phone call from the nanny.
explicacao: Citação de Emily Roberson sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “Every working mother has the things she dreads, things that keep her up in the night – pink eye, …” articula educacao, humor, motherhood em registro de educacao, com relevância para contextos como boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.
